entry.title: Kickstart SAP TechEd 2024 with Autonomous Finance and Maximize Your Business Efficiency
entry.content:
SAP TechEd 2024 is here! As we kick off for this pivotal event in the SAP calendar, anticipation is building around the revolutionary talks and groundbreaking ideas set to be unveiled. For this year’s fully virtual TechEd, I’m excited to share the new SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP) use case, Autonomous Finance.

 

What is Autonomous Finance?

So, what exactly is autonomous finance, and why is it becoming a game-changer in the world of oCFO? Gartner states that autonomous finance is a “finance function where processes and activities are partly governed and majority operated by self-learning software agents that optimize front-, middle- and back-office operations." Powered by technologies like AI, machine learning, and automation, it will affect all parts of the finance function under the office of the CFO. 

So why should businesses be excited about autonomous finance? Let's examine some of its compelling advantages.

  1. Autonomy in finance drastically reduces human intervention, which translates into significant cost reductions for organizations.
  2. It also speeds up operations and boosts productivity.
  3. AI-driven analytics can provide deep, actionable insights from financial data, empowering businesses to make smarter, strategic decisions.

Take a look at this infographic on the trusted path to autonomous finance. 

 

Customer Success Stories

Several SAP customers are already tapping into the potential of Autonomous Finance to propel their businesses forward:

  • Connect departments: Endress+Hauser significantly boosted overall efficiency and simplified operations by utilizing SAP Integration Suite’s tools to standardize their data sources. Thanks to the suite’s APIs and capabilities, Endress+Hauser's customers can now effortlessly place equipment orders directly from their ERP systems. This, in turn, automatically generates a production order within Endress Hauser’s ERP system and forwards it to the production facility. 
    • Aligned priority use case: Deliver Connected Experiences - Accelerate integration and increase productivity
  • Analyze: ARAG adopted SAP Datasphere and SAP Analytics Cloud to create a unified and efficient analytics solution, enabling easy data accessibility. The combination of these technologies enabled ARAG to make all their data more accessible across different countries and teams, which paved the way for faster gaining of meaningful insights.
    • Aligned priority use case 1: Integrate hyperscaler data with SAP Datasphere - Deliver insights using live data from SAP and hyperscalers
    • Aligned priority use case 2: Accelerate Financial Planning and Analysis - Enhance SAP S/4HANA with planning and analysis
  • Automate processes: Orbico's IT team implemented SAP Build Process Automation to automate specific processes and tasks, thereby reducing project times and costs. The team launched over 40 process automation projects within a year, including a bot that automated invoice processing from PDFs into SAP S/4HANA, recognizing invoices from 20 vendors.
    • Aligned priority use case: Increase efficiency and accuracy while reducing costs - Automate processes to empower finance professionals
  • Embed intelligence: To Mercedes-AMG PETRONAS Formula One Team, efficiency is key to success. Formula One has cost caps that limit each team’s spending on cars per season with severe penalties for overshoots, making financial management one of their biggest challenges. Mercedes-AMG PETRONAS Team will leverage SAP S/4HANA Finance and SAP Business AI to optimize resource management and cost forecasting.
    • Aligned priority use case: Enhance customer support using the power of generative AI - Improve operations and decision-making with the power of generative AI

 

How to get started

SAP Discovery Center is an incredible asset to any business. If you’re in search of ways to start deriving value with SAP Business Technology Platform for your business, this is the place to start. It empowers users to easily uncover, find, and embark on SAP Business Technology Platform use cases.

Why SAP Discovery Center?

  1. SAP Discovery Center offers a catalogue of hundreds of detailed and comprehensive missions. These missions cover numerous aspects of business to inspire you on your SAP BTP journey. For example, when you’re ready to embark on the Autonomous Finance journey, these Discovery Center Missions will help you to take each step and make it best suited to your business needs.
  2. It provides guidance on how to use the wide range of services offered by SAP. This step-by-step guidance means you can use SAP’s powerful suite of tools to the best of your abilities and utilize the Free Tier and/or Free Trial.
  3. It encourages learning and exploration. With SAP Discovery Center, you not only understand how a host of innovative technologies could potentially benefit your business but also how to implement them efficiently for the best results with the provided reference architecture.
  4. The collaborative feature of SAP Discovery Center is very important. Knowledge and experiences are shared, thus enhancing productivity and enabling synergy. You can see which existing SAP customers have already implemented the use case of your interest and connect with SAP experts if you have any questions on the Mission.

Using SAP Discovery Center means you’re leveraging not just a tool but a collaborative platform that guides innovation, encourages learning, and boosts efficiency. Make sure you're tuned in for the XP180 | Get started with SAP BTP: Discover and accelerate with SAP Discovery Center session by Nis Boy Naeve and Ingo Deck from SAP Discovery Center team to learn more. 

 

What else can I do with SAP Business Technology Platform?

If you are interested in understanding further what SAP Business Technology Platform can do for your business, check out our comprehensive use case one-pager. The document is designed to provide a clear outline of how SAP BTP can be utilized to enhance your business operations, per your department. Grasping the use cases will allow your business to leverage SAP BTP effectively to drive business performance and inform your strategy, discover new potential, and maximize your technology investment with our SAP BTP use case one-pager. Don’t forget to also check out SAP Business Technology Platform use cases on the website.

You can read further on the SAP Business Technology Platform Priority Use Case One-Pager on this blog article.

 

Get ready for SAP TechEd 2024

I hope this got you excited about SAP TechEd 2024. Don't miss this opportunity to be part of a transformative event, and make sure to register for the keynote session with Muhammad Alam. Register now to secure your spot, and add the following recommended sessions to your agenda.

  • DT100 | Unlocking the intelligent enterprise: Cloud ERP innovations for the future
  • DA180 | Explore SAP Datasphere and SAP Analytics Cloud end to end
  • IN103 | Modernize and elevate to the cloud with SAP Integration Suite
  • XP206 | Achieve enterprise automation with SAP BTP solutions and SAP Signavio
  • AI194 | SAP Business AI and Joule: What’s in it for innovators? 
  • XP180 | Get started with SAP BTP: Discover and accelerate with SAP Discovery Center

 

We look forward to connecting with you!

entry.title: Benefit From SAP Master Data Governance on SAP S/4HANA 2023 Feature Pack Stack 02
entry.content:
Latest Innovations in a Nutshell

Continuing the blog series about updates on MDG innovations in SAP S/4HANA, I'd like to provide you with details on the latest innovations in SAP Master Data Governance on SAP S/4HANA 2023 Feature Pack 02. Highlights include:

  • Generative AI for assisted changes/summary of changes in central governance (classic mode) and for business partner processing (in cloud-ready mode) using Joule AI copilot.
  • Improved data quality analytics for business partner and product master data.
  • Enrichment of business partner from CDQ reference data​ as an integrated part of consolidation, mass processing, etc.

These come in addition to various continuous improvements in central governance (classic mode).

Innovations in Detail

Let's take a closer look at the innovations.

Inclusion of Generative AI in MDG

Generative AI is used for summary of changes and assisted changes in central governance (classic mode), and for optional business partner processing (in cloud-ready mode) initiated by a Joule dialog. 

The AI-generated summary of changes provides you with an overview of changes in natural language (using a large language model) enabling you to: ​

  • verify the completeness and/or correctness of master data changes.​
  • get a brief overview of the changes comprised in an MDG change request.​
  • avoid pointless revisions based on incomplete changes.​
  • have a non-technical summary of changes (in contrast to change documents).​
  • facilitate approver’s final checks.

Generative AI-based summary of changes

​The AI-assisted change lets you change master data attributes with as little effort and as few steps as possible so that applying changes to a master data object is time efficient. You can make prompts in natural language to make field changes.

The AI-assisted change in central governance is applicable for business partner and material, and the AI-generated summary of changes in central governance is applicable for business partner, material, and custom objects which do not use editions.

In addition, SAP Master Data Governance on SAP S/4HANA integrates with the AI copilot Joule, enabling queries in natural language and navigation to SAP Master Data Governance on SAP S/4HANA cloud-ready mode for follow-up actions on business partner (incl. supplier and customer) data. (This innovation can be consumed as of FPS01).

Joule integration with follow-ups in MDG (cloud-ready mode)

These initial steps of including Generative AI in MDG processes are planned to be continued to cover more use cases in MDG classic mode and cloud-ready mode. 

"Data Quality Analytics 2.0" for business partner and product master data

  • SAP Analytics Cloud comes with an enhanced dashboard for chief data officers or master data specialists to get a real-time quality abstract on the prevailing quality and history trends of product and business partner master data, providing:
    • data quality overview in 3-level reporting structure by category, dimension, and rule
    • data quality analytics for seven business partner segments, e.g., Business Partner Core Data, Customer Sales Data, and Supplier Purchasing Data, each with drill-down using master data attributes or/and organization unit e.g., Country, Company Codes etc.
    • data quality analytics for seven product master data segments, e.g., Plant data, Sales Data, and Classification Data, each with drill-down using master data attributes or/and organization unit e.g., Product Group, Plant etc.
    • embedded authorization control via live data connection
    • integration in MDG system for data remediation

Data quality overview in 3-level reporting structure: by category, dimension, and ruleDeep-dive data quality analytics on business partner in SAP Analytics Cloud

For more details about "Data Quality Analytics 2.0", read the SAP Community blog post by Kefeng Wang.

  • Analytics CDS views for master data quality of business partner and product provide data source (CDS views) for master data quality analytics of business partner and product using organizational unit and attributes in SAP S/4HANA.

Enrichment of business partner records from reference data as an integrated part of consolidation, mass processing, etc.

In cloud-ready mode, the enrichment of business partner records from CDQ reference data is enabled as an integrated and automated step within:

  • Single Processing​
  • Mass Processing​
  • Consolidation​
  • Federation processes

Mass enrichment of business partners with reference data in SAP S/4HANA

For more information about the enrichment of business partner records from reference data with full process integration, read the SAP Community blog post by Martin Steinhauser.

Integration of SAP Business Workflow as local workflow engine (cloud-ready mode)

As an alternative to BTP Workflow Service (subscription required), the SAP Business Workflow can be assigned to process steps in the process template configuration. It can trigger any custom-defined action after a process step (e.g. email notification).

Integrate SAP Business Workflow as local workflow engine (cloud-ready mode)

Continued improvements for central governance in classic mode (material data)

Increased effectiveness through enhanced copy function in search and use of validation rules for classification in central governance for material: 

  • You can copy a selected material from the Search Materials Result List and choose one of three different copying options: Only Basic Data, All Data, or By Organizational Levels. When selecting Only Basic Data, basic data, long texts, and classification are copied. Choosing All Data will result in the complete data from the template material being copied. If you select By Organization Level, a dialog box will appear allowing you to choose which organizational data should be copied.​ 

Copy function in material search
Additionally, if a change request exists for the template material, the changed data from staging will be included in the copied material.

  • You can use validation rules for classes (table KSSK) and characteristics (table AUSP), defined using the rule management functionality of MDG, data quality management to check data during change request processing.

Validation rules for classes and characteristics

Continued improvements for central governance in classic mode (business partner incl. customer and supplier data)

SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS02 comes with improved Partner Functions content display and relationship categories in business partner processing:

  • The relationship type allows you to classify the relationship category into different subdivisions. Accordingly, you can maintain the relationship type on the user interface for Relationships, Contact Persons, Shareholder Relationships, and their related details user interface.
  • In addition, there are more search criteria when using the F4 Help on the Partner field in the partner functions lists ERP Customer Sales Areas: Partner Functions or ERP Vendor Purchasing Organization: Partner Functions. The F4 help also includes Business Partner, Customer, Supplier and Maximum No. of Hits as search criteria. The UIBBs for Partner Functions include the column Assigned BP. 

UIBB Partner Functions

Maintaining the Relation Type

For an overview of the innovations outlined above, see also the What's New in SAP Master Data Governance on SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS 2 presentation. As you can see from these innovations, both classic mode in MDG and cloud-ready mode are continuously enhanced. The driver for innovations in classic mode is primarily the SAP Continuous Influence program, whereas cloud-ready mode in MDG is the strategic development path going forward.

To get the full picture of planned innovations in SAP Master Data Governance across the entire MDG solution portfolio up to 2025, see the SAP Roadmap Explorer.

To stay up to date, simply follow the SAP Community Topic Page for SAP Master Data Governance and join the Q&A and discussion forum.

entry.title: SAP S/4 Hana - Zero Downtime Option
entry.content:
I am working on Release Upgrade from S/4 Hana 2021 to SAP S/4 Hana 2023, I’ve been diving into the Zero Downtime Option (ZDO) and wanted to make it easier for everyone to understand this game-changing approach for software updates.

Why ZDO?

System downtime during updates can be costly and disruptive. Imagine upgrading your system without halting operations—ZDO makes that possible! It allows updates for SAP S/4 HANA without technical downtime by keeping users working seamlessly while the update happens in the background.

How ZDO Works?

  1. Up-time Processing starts with your original system (Release 1). Users work as usual.
  2. Once ZDO is initiated, users transition smoothly to a bridge subsystem, which mimics the source system while the update is prepared in the background.
  3. Transactional Consistency is maintained throughout the process, ensuring your data stays intact.
  4. The target release (Release 2) is then activated, with minimal disruption. After a short restart (just 5-15 minutes), users are moved to the new release without them even realising it.
    Key Benefit
  • Minimal downtime: A single restart, no database reboot.
  • Low resource footprint: Only necessary tables are cloned.
  • No extra licenses or tools required.

ZDO not only reduces downtime but also helps keep business operations flowing seamlessly, ensuring system availability while upgrades happen in parallel

#sap #sapcommunity #SAPS/4HanaUpgrades #S/4HANA2021TO2023 #S4Hana @#S/4Hana

entry.title: Maximizing M&A Success: How SAP Solutions Drive Growth and Effectiveness
entry.content:
Maximizing M&A Success:  How SAP Solutions Drive Growth and Effectiveness

Authored by:  Christina Grace, SAP Global Finance Value Advisor & Marie Trojani, SAP Global HCM Value Advisor

Mergers and acquisitions (M&A) are complex transactions that can significantly alter the trajectory of a business. While they present opportunities for growth, diversification, and enhanced market presence, they also come with inherent challenges that can hinder success. One critical factor in navigating these challenges is the effective integration of systems, processes, people, and cultures. SAP offers a robust framework that can significantly enhance a company’s effectiveness for companies pursuing an M&A strategy. This blog explores how SAP facilitates successful M&A outcomes in the areas of Finance and HR and discusses potential inflection points in the journey to ensure success.

Understanding the M&A Landscape

Before delving into how SAP can assist during the M&A process, it's important to understand the typical M&A landscape. The process generally involves several phases:

  1. Planning and Strategy Development: Identifying potential targets and assessing strategic fit. This includes an assessment of current systems, people, and processes in both organizations. The goal should be to identify gaps and overlaps that could impact integration.  This phase also involves engaging key stakeholders from both organizations to ensure buy-in and prepare teams for the upcoming changes.
  2. Due Diligence: Analyzing financials, operations, and risks associated with the target company.  This analysis should provide insights into the target company’s strengths and weaknesses.  During this phase, data management tools can be used to perform scenario modeling of the two companies, set KPI goals, measure compliance, and ensure data harmonization.  A risk assessment should be performed to evaluate potential risks associated with the acquisition. Identifying risks early allows for proactive management strategies. That’s also at this stage that you can evaluate the cultural fit between the two companies.
  3. Integration and Strategy Management: Merging systems, processes, people, and cultures post-acquisition. Develop a comprehensive integration roadmap that outlines timelines, responsibilities, and key milestones. This roadmap should leverage GenAI integration capabilities to ensure a seamless transition. Prepare a training and support plan to educate employees about new systems and processes. Effective training will minimize disruptions and foster a sense of ownership among staff.  Real-time monitoring of the integration process is essential and allows for quick adjustments if any issues arise.  Establish feedback mechanisms to gather input from employees about the integration process. Utilize these insights to refine strategies and address concerns promptly. Particularly, HR concerns can appear quickly around job redundancies, salary gaps, talent retention for critical roles and employee morale.
  4. Post-Merger Evaluation: Assessing the success of the integration and overall M&A performance. After integration, evaluate the performance of the combined entity using advanced analytics tools. Assess whether M&A goals have been met and identify areas for further improvement, realizing greater synergies, and operating more efficiently.  Perform a cultural integration review by assessing employee engagement and satisfaction.  This allows companies to gauge the success of cultural integration, to gather insights, and to address any lingering issues.

Each phase presents unique challenges that SAP addresses with agility, transparency, and scalability.

The Role of SAP in M&A

SAP provides a comprehensive suite of integrated solutions designed to enhance operational efficiency, drive revenue growth, harmonize data, and improve real-time insights and overall business intelligence. Here’s how SAP Finance and HR solutions can specifically help companies pursuing an M&A strategy:

Seamless Integration of Systems

Challenge: One of the biggest hurdles in M&A is integrating disparate IT systems from merging organizations. Incompatible systems can lead to inefficiencies, data silos, and disruptions in business operations.

SAP Solution: SAP’s integrated platform enables organizations to unify disparate systems under one umbrella. With tools such as SAP S/4HANA, businesses can achieve real-time data integration and consistency, allowing for streamlined operations across the newly formed entity.  In the HR community, harmonizing different payroll, benefits, and performance management systems requires strategic planning and meticulous execution to avoid disruptions and maintain employee satisfaction.  This seamless integration reduces downtime and ensures continuity of service, which is critical during transitional phases.

Efficient Data Management

Challenge: During an M&A integration, organizations face an overwhelming amount of data that needs to be migrated, analyzed, and integrated. Poor data management can result in costly mistakes and compliance issues.

SAP Solution: SAP’s data management tools, including SAP Business Technology Platform, provide robust capabilities for data migration, cleansing, and integration. These solutions ensure that data from both companies are harmonized and accessible, allowing decision-makers to derive insights quickly and effectively. Real-time analytics further enable businesses to identify trends and make informed decisions swiftly.

Streamlined Financial Reporting

Challenge: Accurate financial reporting is essential during M&A, both for compliance and for assessing the financial health of the combined entity. However, multiple financial systems and reporting standards can complicate this process.

SAP Solution: SAP solutions offer integrated financial management tools that facilitate streamlined reporting and compliance with international financial standards. With SAP Analytics Cloud, businesses can create real-time financial dashboards, enabling stakeholders to monitor KPIs and other financial metrics seamlessly. This capability is crucial for transparent communication with investors, internal stakeholders, and regulatory bodies.

Enhanced Operational Efficiency

Challenge: Post-M&A, organizations often encounter operational redundancies and inefficiencies due to overlapping processes and systems.  Furthermore, redundancies and restructuring can lead to workforce reductions, which may result in the loss of valuable talent and institutional knowledge.

SAP Solution: SAP’s process automation capabilities can help identify and eliminate redundancies. For example, with SAP Ariba, companies can enhance procurement processes, while SAP SuccessFactors can streamline human resources management. HR must manage these transitions delicately, ensuring fair and compliant processes while maintaining operational continuity. These solutions foster operational efficiencies that lead to cost savings and improved productivity across the organization. 

Change Management Support

Challenge: M&A transactions often lead to significant challenges to company culture, processes, and personnel. Concerns about the unknown and resistance to change can derail integration efforts. 

SAP Solution:  Fostering employee engagement and loyalty can be key to overcoming these obstacles. Initiatives to support change management, such as training programs and team-building activities, are essential but must be carefully tailored to address the specific needs and concerns of the workforce.  SAP offers tools to support change management, including SAP Enable Now, which provides training and support resources for employees.  This tool allows organizations to create custom training materials and resources that can facilitate a smoother transition. By preparing employees for the changes ahead, companies can mitigate resistance and foster a more collaborative environment.

Risk Management and Compliance

Challenge: M&A transactions are inherently risky, and the complexities of integrating different compliance standards can lead to potential legal and regulatory issues.

SAP Solution: SAP’s risk management tools, such as SAP GRC (Governance, Risk, and Compliance), enable businesses to assess and manage risks effectively. These solutions provide a framework for identifying compliance gaps and ensuring adherence to regulatory requirements, thereby protecting the organization from potential liabilities.

Cultural Integration

Challenge: Merging different company cultures can be one of the most challenging aspects of M&A. Misalignment can lead to employee dissatisfaction and turnover.  M&A often herald significant organizational transformation, presenting numerous challenges for HR and employees. Merging distinct corporate cultures can lead to misalignment, confusion, and conflicts. HR must navigate the complex task of creating a cohesive environment where employees from both organizations feel valued and understood.

SAP Solution: While SAP solutions primarily focus on operational aspects, they can also support cultural integration indirectly. Tools like SAP SuccessFactors provide insights into employee engagement and performance, enabling leadership to address cultural challenges proactively. By fostering open communication and collaboration through these platforms, organizations can work towards aligning their cultures more effectively.

Job Security

Challenge: Another critical issue is job security. Employees often face anxiety and uncertainty about their future roles, leading to decreased morale and productivity. There is a higher risk for top talent to leave the company during times of uncertainty.

SAP Solution: Transparent communication from HR is vital to mitigate these concerns, as well as a comprehensive view into employee data such as competencies and job profiles. Analytics and AI can facilitate informed decisions and proactively alert if there are potential risks. Yet finding the balance between transparency and confidentiality can be challenging.

In navigating all of these challenges, Finance, HR and IT all play a crucial role in facilitating a smooth transition, fostering a positive organizational culture, and maintaining employee well-being and productivity during M&A activities.

Benefits of SAP Solutions in M&A

  • Efficiency: Streamlines operations and reduces integration time
  • Cost Savings: Minimizes redundancies and operational costs
  • Real-Time Insights: Provides data-driven decision-making capabilities
  • Risk Mitigation: Enhances compliance and governance
  • Employee Engagement: Supports smooth cultural integration through training and engagement tools

Conclusion

In today’s fast-paced business environment, the success of M&A hinges on effective integration and management of systems, people, data, and processes. In an era where adaptability and efficiency are paramount, embracing SAP Finance and HR solutions not only supports immediate M&A goals but also lays a solid foundation for sustained success in an ever-evolving marketplace.

SAP offers a comprehensive toolkit for companies navigating the multifaceted complexities and challenges of M&A, enabling organizations to streamline operations, enhance decision-making, and drive overall success.  By leveraging SAP’s integrated tools, companies can navigate the complexities of M&A with greater confidence and effectiveness. By facilitating integration, enhancing data management, and supporting operational efficiency, SAP helps organizations achieve their M&A objectives while laying the groundwork for long-term success.  As organizations continue to pursue growth through acquisitions, those that adopt SAP solutions will be well-positioned to realize the full potential of their M&A strategies, fostering a more agile and competitive future.

entry.title: SAP Commerce Cloud Q3 ‘24 Release Highlights
entry.content:
In Q3 2024, we've been hard at work to expand and enhance our unified commerce solution with new capabilities, including new AI functionality as well as core platform improvements. This will ensure that our customers operate in a secure, compliant and scalable manner, allowing them to focus their energy on their business. From zone redundancies to self-service premium disaster recovery, these backend improvements add an extra layer of stability and resilience, ensuring your commerce operations run without a hitch.

Our new AI functionalities are aimed at boosting efficiency. We've added AI Image Generation to the SAP CX AI Toolkit and announced the ChannelEngine partnership at SAP CX Live for AI-powered marketplace management. We'll delve deeper into this exciting partnership in this post.

The SAP CX Live virtual event also showcased how our comprehensive SAP Customer Experience portfolio helps your customers get more of what they want. For an in-depth look at the business advantages of using SAP CX solutions with SAP ERP, check out the new paper, Analyzing the Economic Benefits of Using AI-Powered SAP CX Solutions Natively Connected to SAP ERP.

Now, let's explore the standout features we've introduced over the past three months for SAP Commerce Cloud. Don't miss our Q3 2024 Release Highlights webcast for all the latest innovations.

 

Insightful

Merchandising your products just got easier with AI

Have you ever considered using AI to automatically create product images to show to customers? Wouldn’t that save you time and resources? Well, SAP Commerce Cloud merchandising capabilities just got even better with the addition of the Product Image Designer. The AI Image Generation tool lets you automatically create captivating new product images from simple text descriptions. Plus, the Background Editor allows you to transform or remove existing product image backgrounds, staying on-brand and boosting engagement with visuals tailored for commerce catalogs.

These enhancements are designed to make your life easier, letting you focus on what you do best while ensuring your product images look top-notch!

Product Image Designer

 

Adaptive

Boosting resiliency through enhanced cloud operations

SAP Commerce Cloud provides global hosting on Azure, where the platform can be hosted in your region of choice. While each region is divided into multiple availability zones, what happens if one availability zone has a disruption? This is where zone redundancies and Premium Disaster Recovery come in.

The perks of zone redundancies include bolstered resilience and security for your data centers, greater reliability for clusters, and increased availability for critical workloads such as search and storefront operations.

In Q2, we rolled out zone redundancies for Azure SQL databases. This feature automatically replicates databases across different zones within the same region, ensuring that a disruption won’t jeopardize your production environment.

For Q3, we’ve added zone redundancies for the SAP Commerce Cloud application along with key infrastructure, such as compute and load balancers. Keep an eye out for zone redundancies on storage which is coming soon!

We are thrilled to also announce that Premium Disaster Recovery is now available for self-service purchase in SAP Commerce Cloud through your Consumption Credits, a "virtual currency" we introduced earlier this year. You can log in to your cloud portal and assess this feature’s applicability in your enterprise through the updated estimator tool. From there, you can subsequently schedule and manage your upgrade from Standard to Premium Disaster Recovery.

Our systems are already designed with full backup capability in case of a major disruption. If a black swan event were to occur, Premium Disaster Recovery dramatically reduces both the Recovery Point Objective (RPO) and Recovery Time Objective (RTO). In a nutshell, RPO is the amount of data that would be lost, while RTO is the length of time it takes to recover and fully be back online. With Premium Disaster Recovery, the data loss and maximum amount of time to be back to regular online operations are both reduced by at least 75%.

These improvements aim to give you greater control and peace of mind, ensuring your business can bounce back swiftly from any disruptions.

Generate shareable links at a click of a button with Backoffice Deep Linking

Last year, we introduced Backoffice deep linking, and we're excited to take it up a notch with automated link generation. Now, you can easily generate URLs to bookmark and navigate directly to specific pages and items within Backoffice.

Share these links effortlessly with your team, agencies, and other colleagues to boost productivity and collaboration. This update will save you the hassle of manually navigating to specific pages every time. Enjoy a smoother, more efficient workflow!

Improve customer service and site security with Composable Storefront enhancements

Our Composable Storefront now comes with exciting new features designed to elevate customer service and enhance site security.

The Assisted Service Module has been updated to help your customer service agents shine. With just one click, agents can navigate from the customer list to a selected customer's 360-degree view. Switching back to the customer list to check other details is just as seamless. Additionally, the module now allows agents to search using the order ID and apply site filtering, making it a breeze to view order details and product pages for customers.

On the security front, the Composable Storefront now includes a verification step (think "I am not a robot") during customer registration to bolster site security. This comes with preconfigured support for Google reCAPTCHA v2. This added layer of security prevents brute-force attacks in registration and prevents bots from spamming websites.

These updates are all about making customer interactions smoother and more secure, making it a win-win for everyone!

 

Connected

Get a better handle over your order lifecycle with the latest SAP Order Management Services update

We're excited to introduce an option for synchronous integration between SAP Commerce Cloud and SAP Order Management Services that allow for faster and more efficient order processing, real time visibility and reduced overall operational costs.

To further assist you with managing large orders, we've also launched workflow loops. This feature simplifies orchestration by allowing you to apply sophisticated workflows to an even broader set of order data with significantly fewer steps.

Additionally, we've introduced the ability to include reasons for any Order Activity types, such as the rationale behind a delivery failure. This functionality is available for all standard and custom Order Activity types. Reason analysis is a crucial step towards optimizing the order lifecycle, and we're here to support you in achieving that goal.

Loop Control workflow for SAP Order Management Services

Harness AI to optimize marketplace management across 950+ sales channels

The SAP Store has expanded with new innovations and services, enriching our digital experience portfolio. We're thrilled to spotlight our partner, ChannelEngine, which empowers customers to connect and sell products on over 950 different online sales channels and international marketplaces. This solution is designed to maximize conversion rates, increase average order value, and boost customer engagement.

ChannelEngine provides brands, retailers, and distributors with the advanced technical capabilities needed to navigate the complexities of commerce management. By combining the strengths of SAP OMS and ChannelEngine, you can operate commerce unbounded across the globe, with centralized shopper order processing and access to rich insights into consumer demand, sales performance, and market trends. This partnership helps enhance unified commerce decision-making, making your business smarter and more agile.

ChannelEngine connects with 950+ online sales channels

 For more details on the above release features, check out the following resources:

  • Q3 2024 SAP Commerce Cloud Release Readiness Kit
  • Performance Optimization Best Practices
  • SAP Commerce Cloud update cycle
  • SAP Commerce Cloud Release Navigator single access point to release content

SAP Help Portal

  • SAP Commerce Cloud What’s New

Please share your feedback in a comment and follow our SAP Commerce Cloud topic page to connect with the community!

entry.title: 📢 Transition from TS410 to Cloud-Based IE2EE
entry.content:
SAP University Alliances member institutions have long offered SAP certification to highlight students' SAP expertise. The "Integrated Business Processes in SAP S/4HANA (TS410)" course will retire on June 30, 2025 and be replaced by "End-to-End Business Processes for the Intelligent Enterprise (IE2EE)" with SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud, which is now available. SAP University Alliances will provide next steps for users of TS410. 

📢 For new certifications, start with the cloud-based course

entry.title: DTV New Release Announcement: DTV 1.5 released to customers
entry.content:
I am glad to announce that DTV 1.5 (latest and greatest DTV version) is now generally available to our customers with some NEW features such as partial executions, work process utilization, enhanced result data, copy functionality, execution runtime and performance improvements.

These latest features would further augment the business data validation process with ease, speed and accuracy using the DTV tool.

Key Features

  1. Partial Executions: You can now execute Simulation, Extraction, and Evaluation individually at the work item level.
  2. Work Process Utilization: The tool allows you to set a maximum number of work processes, limiting the system resource consumption.
  3. Enhanced Result Data: All business report results, including hidden fields, are available in extraction and evaluation with filtered queries.
  4. Copy Functionality: Enhanced usability by allowing to copy the entire test specification and project global data.
  5. Execution Runtimes: Accurate execution runtime calculations are done by considering delays.
  6. Performance Improvement: Evaluation performance has been optimized to handle data sizes larger than 2GB.

For more details:

  • SAP Note 3117879 – Central SAP Note for DTV
  • SAP Note 3511932 – DTV Tool 1.5 delivery
  • Help Documentation – Data Transition Validation

Related Articles:

Business data validation using DTV tool - SAP Community

Best practices to follow during DTV implementation - SAP Community

Business Data Validation of Custom Reports using D... - SAP Community

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS2: What’s in it for the Utilities Industry?
entry.content:
You might have seen my previous articles emphasizing the main innovations dedicated to the utilities industry that are new with SAP S/4HANA release 2023, including its first feature pack (FPS1):

SAP S/4HANA 2023: What’s in it for the Utilities Industry? 
SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS1: What’s in it for the Utilities Industry? 

Now it's time to talk about FPS2 - the second feature pack available for the 2023 release in accordance with the SAP S/4HANA release strategy:

  1. One of the major innovations available with SAP S/4HANA Utilities are the numerous SAP Fiori apps tailored to the needs of the billing specialist, the meter reading specialist and the operations specialist roles. With this new shipment they offer direct navigation to the both comprehensive data environment trees, showing business and technical master data in hierarchical views: Business Partner Overview and Premise Overview:


    For more details see Direct navigation to data environments from SAP Fiori apps in utilities meter reading and billing or SAP Help Portal for a list of all enabled navigation options.

  2. As mentioned in my previous blog post published in Q1 2024, SAP continues working on a more native integration between SAP S/4HANA Utilities and SAP Datasphere, as the next generation of SAP Data Warehouse Cloud. In the context of SAP's analytics roadmap, SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS2 offers the extraction of further utilities master data – such as Devices and Points of Delivery (PoDs) as well as important transactional data from meter reading – to SAP Datasphere. For details see roadmap item Leveraging of SAP Datasphere for the utilities industry – device, PoD, transactional data.
    In addition to that, important business KPIs based on queries of SAP S/4HANA Utilities are now available in SAP Analytics Cloud, for further information see     Leveraging SAP Analytics Cloud for the utilities industry .

  3. The integration of SAP S/4HANA sales orders with utilities commodity contracts - initially delivered as Sales Interface Integration one year ago - has been enhanced through SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS2 to cover additional contract life cycle events such as change start date, change end date and withdraw contract. You can also now provide a rejection season for sales orders.

    Moreover, in the context of sales integration, new customizing options allow a more accurate billing simulation for new commodity products as well as a more accurate product search:

    For details see Extended integration of sales orders with utilities commodity contracts and Advanced product search, simulation, and comparison.

  4. Following the Customer Fields and Logic concept of SAP NetWeaver, it is now easier to add your own additional attributes to the SAP Utilities contract, see Enabling custom fields and logic for utilities contract master data. Key-user extensibility is one element of SAP's Clean Core strategy, see SAP note 3406389 for a comprehensive overview on Clean Core for SAP S/4HANA Utilities.

  5. Invoicing as part of S/4HANA Utilities has now been integrated with SAP Digital Payments Add-On, offering customers of utility companies a more convenient way to pay their utilities bill, for instance using a QR code on their invoice. For details see Integration of payment by link for utilities invoices.

  6. In accordance with German legal guidelines (§19 Stromgrundversorgungsverordnung), SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS2 helps German default suppliers to offer a special forms of installment plans to their customers to prevent a disconnection being performed. For details see Abwendungsvereinbarung (Prevention Agreement in Germany). In addition to that, a new basic device category called "control box" was introduced to support legal requirements (§14a Energiewirtschaftsgesetz) in Germany, to learn more see Support for specific utilities devices like control boxes as well as SAP note 3423201.

  7. Finally SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS2 also comprises multiple enhancements to SAP S/4HANA Utilities which have been implemented based on improvement requests submitted within the SAP Continuous Influence session dedicated to SAP S/4HANA Utilities: https://influence.sap.com/go/s4utilities  
    For all details see https://community.sap.com/t5/sap-for-utilities-blogs/sap-continuous-influence-session-for-sap-s-4hana-utilities-status-update/ba-p/13740619 or the following roadmap items:
    • Change load profile values in billed periods and replace values for profiles without value-replacement for enhancements related to component IS-U-EDM (energy data management)
    • Ability to disable obsolete service providers for enhancements related to component IS-U-MD (master data)
    • Simplified reversal of full device replacement, more information for meter readings, and order creation for new devices for enhancements related to component IS-U-DM (device management) and IS-U-DM-MR (meter readings)

entry.title: Automating SAP Post-Refresh Steps with SAP GUI Scripting (Part 2)
entry.content:
In Part 1, I introduced the basics of automating SAP post-refresh tasks using SAP GUI scripting. In Part 2, we will dive into more advanced automation tasks, focusing on key transactions (T-codes) that simplify common post-refresh activities. Each T-code is paired with a scripting example to show how these steps can be automated efficiently. 

T-Code: SM35 - Batch Input Monitoring (Job Log Deletion)

How It Helps: Post-refresh, outdated job logs need to be removed to prevent conflicts or unnecessary storage usage. Manually deleting job logs can be tedious, especially in larger systems. Automating this step ensures that the system starts clean after the refresh.

Script Example:

session.findById("wnd[0]/tbar[0]/okcd").text = "/nsm35"
session.findById("wnd[0]").sendVKey 0
session.findById("wnd[0]/usr/btn%_UABLG_%_APP_%-VALU_PUSH").press
session.findById("wnd[0]/tbar[1]/btn[16]").press  ' Deletes job logs

T-Code: SU01 - User Management (Password Update)

How It Helps: User passwords often need to be reset after a system refresh to maintain security and access control. Automating this process is critical when managing multiple user accounts.

Script Example:

session.findById("wnd[0]/tbar[0]/okcd").text = "/nsu01"
session.findById("wnd[0]").sendVKey 0
session.findById("wnd[0]/usr/ctxtSUID_ST_BNAME-LOW").text = "USERNAME"
session.findById("wnd[0]/usr/btn%_BUTTON").press  ' Updates password

T-Code: BDLS - Conversion of Logical System Names

How It Helps: After a system copy or refresh, logical system names need to be updated to reflect the new environment. Automating the BDLS conversion reduces the risk of errors and ensures that all necessary updates are completed efficiently.

Script Example:

session.findById("wnd[0]/tbar[0]/okcd").text = "/nbdls"
session.findById("wnd[0]").sendVKey 0
session.findById("wnd[0]/usr/txtBDLS_FROM").text = "OLD_LOGSYS"
session.findById("wnd[0]/usr/txtBDLS_TO").text = "NEW_LOGSYS"
session.findById("wnd[0]/tbar[1]/btn[8]").press  ' Start BDLS Conversion

T-Code: DBCO - Database Connections Maintenance

How It Helps: After a refresh, database connections need to be re-established. Automating this process ensures that connections are properly configured, reducing the risk of connection issues post-refresh.

Script Example:

session.findById("wnd[0]/tbar[0]/okcd").text = "/ndbco"
session.findById("wnd[0]").sendVKey 0
session.findById("wnd[0]/usr/btn%_CONNECTION_%_APP_%-VALU_PUSH").press
session.findById("wnd[0]/usr/txtDBCOTXT-CONN").text = "CONNECTION_NAME"
session.findById("wnd[0]/usr/txtDBCOTXT-USER").text = "USERNAME"
session.findById("wnd[0]/usr/txtDBCOTXT-PASS").text = "PASSWORD"
session.findById("wnd[0]/tbar[1]/btn[11]").press  ' Save DB Connection

T-Code: SMLG - Logon Group Configuration

How It Helps: Configuring logon groups helps distribute the user workload across multiple servers. Automating this process ensures that the correct logon groups are set up after the refresh, optimizing system performance.

Script Example:

session.findById("wnd[0]/tbar[0]/okcd").text = "/nsmlg"
session.findById("wnd[0]").sendVKey 0
session.findById("wnd[0]/usr/ctxtGROUP_NAME").text = "LOGON_GROUP"
session.findById("wnd[0]/usr/ctxtSERVER_NAME").text = "SERVER_NAME"
session.findById("wnd[0]/tbar[1]/btn[11]").press  ' Save Logon Group

T-Code: ST03 - Workload Analysis

How It Helps: Running workload analysis helps identify performance bottlenecks in the system after a refresh. Automating the ST03 transaction helps streamline the analysis, ensuring that performance data is available immediately.

Script Example:

session.findById("wnd[0]/tbar[0]/okcd").text = "/nst03"
session.findById("wnd[0]").sendVKey 0
session.findById("wnd[0]/usr/btn%_ANALYZE_%").press  ' Start workload analysis

T-Code: TEMSE - TemSe Object Management

How It Helps: Managing TemSe objects, which store spool data, is crucial to avoid performance issues. Automating the cleanup or management of TemSe objects ensures smooth spool processing post-refresh.

Script Example:

session.findById("wnd[0]/tbar[0]/okcd").text = "/ntemse"
session.findById("wnd[0]").sendVKey 0
session.findById("wnd[0]/usr/btn%_MANAGE_TEMSE_%").press  ' Manage TemSe objects

T-Code: Import PSEs

How It Helps: After a system refresh, importing PSEs (Personal Security Environment) for secure communication may be necessary. Automating this ensures that security settings are applied correctly and without manual errors.

Script Example:

session.findById("wnd[0]/tbar[0]/okcd").text = "/nimport_pse"
session.findById("wnd[0]").sendVKey 0
session.findById("wnd[0]/usr/btn%_IMPORT_%").press  ' Import PSEs

 

Conclusion

By automating key post-refresh tasks in SAP using GUI scripting, you can significantly reduce manual efforts, minimize errors, and accelerate the system stabilization process. This guide has provided detailed examples of automating crucial T-codes like SMLG, DBCO, BDLS, and more, to streamline post-refresh activities. These scripts not only help ensure consistency across the system but also enable administrators to focus on more strategic tasks by automating repetitive and time-consuming actions.

entry.title: Incorporating Custom Events from Lobby into SAP Build Process Automation
entry.content:
Introduction

What is SAP Build? 

  • SAP Build is a cloud-based tool for designing and prototyping applications with ease
  • User-friendly interface and drag-and-drop functionality make interactive prototypes without extensive coding
  • Supports real-time collaboration, seamless integration with other SAP solutions, and pre-built templates

In this blog, our goal is to explore the process of uploading event specifications to SAP Build Lobby and consuming those events in SBPA.

SAP Build Process Automation (SBPA) enables business users to automate workflow processes and tasks without writing code.

Check here for What's New and Learn more from the roadmap.  

This August 2024, SAP Build Process Automation has a released a new way of discovering Business Events from the Lobby.

Check out this blog...

Discovering Business Events from Lobby for SAP Build Process Automation: Manage Credit Memo Request 


Creating Event Projects from the Lobby: By Uploading Event Specification...

  • Navigate to the Connector Section in the Lobby:

First, you can access the Lobby section of SAP Build and navigate to the Connector area. This is where you will be able to manage and consume events within your application.

  • Events Have a Dedicated Place and Subpage:

Once in the Connector section, you will find that events have their dedicated place and subpage. This makes it easier for you to manage and work with different types of events for your application.

  • In this guide, we will focus on the Upload specification source when adding an event.
    As a user, you will see the create button on the Events subpage.

  • Events can be easily uploaded into the lobby of our platform.
    Presently, we support only JSON format for event specifications, with plans to expand to additional formats in the future.

For demonstration purposes, I have downloaded an example from BAH to illustrate this process. 

  1. Go to the Business Accelerator Hub page. 
  2. Select any of the products --> for this example -  SAP S/4 HANA Cloud Public Edition 
  3. Select events on the tab below the Title and Short description.
    1. Select any event object that you wish --> for this example - Business Situation Events



  4. Download the file to your system. 

Any events in JSON format can be added, considering that the event mesh supports that specific event.

  • Event spec file uploaded will consists of Events and the Payload of Events:

    Each uploaded event object contains the event itself and its associated payload. This information is crucial for understanding the type and scope of each event that you are consuming.






  • Add the Event Object to the Build Lobby:

    Once you upload the desired event, you can easily view it in the Build Lobby.
    This will make the event accessible within your application.

 

  • Events can be Used Across Build Products:

    Currently, the Build Process Automation is utilizing uploaded events, with plans for Build Apps to also make use of uploaded events in the near future. This means that once you upload an event in the Lobby, it can potentially be utilized across different SAP Build products.

  • Discovering Events via Library in the Process Builder:

    After uploading an event in the Lobby, you can discover and utilize it through the library in the Process Builder. This ensures that the event is easily accessible for building and automating processes within your application.


 

Other use cases…

There are some additional steps to setup Event Mesh 
Event Mesh and SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud Setup for SAP Build Process Automation

entry.title: LEVEREDGE 2024 Highlights: Discovering New Strategies for Digital Transformation
entry.content:
Organizations face a multitude of challenges today, from adapting to rapid technological advancements to navigating complex regulatory environments. LEVEREDGE 2024 was created to address these pressing issues and to provide a platform where industry leaders come together to explore the latest strategies, technologies, and solutions shaping the future of business.

Over three days, this premier event gathered 400+ guests and thought leaders from 10+ countries to share insights, experiences, and success stories. Attendees had the opportunity to engage in diverse sessions, including keynote presentations, panel discussions, and interactive workshops, covering vital topics such as Business AI, Digital Supply Chain, Intelligent Spend & Procurement, Data Migration, S/4HANA, Product Lifecycle Management, and much more.

Key Highlights of Day 1

The event kicked off with a Design Thinking Networking Breakfast, setting the tone for meaningful connections among participants. This engaging atmosphere fostered discussions that sparked new ideas and collaborative solutions.

The day continued with Design Thinking Workshops led by the LeverX AppHaus Team and Andreas Hauser, Senior Vice President of the SAP AppHaus Network, where participants explored AI-driven solutions.

Further collaborative discussions emphasized the potential of AI and fostered a spirit of collaboration, encouraging attendees to address the challenges businesses face on their digital transformation journeys.

Key Highlights of Day 2

Day 2 of LEVEREDGE 2024 was all about deepening our understanding of the latest advancements in digital transformation, artificial intelligence, and innovative business practices.

Spanning three dynamic stages — the Blue Stage, Green Stage, and the Yellow Stage (SAP PLM Forum) — the agenda featured keynotes from industry leaders, expert panels, and interactive discussions, each offering valuable perspectives on the evolving role of technology in today’s business landscape. From AI-powered solutions to groundbreaking PLM innovations, the day was packed with insightful conversations, practical takeaways, and plenty of opportunities to network with peers and thought leaders.

 

🔵 Blue Stage highlights

Opening remarks: Motivational speeches from Dr. Victor Lozinski, Co-Founder and Chairman of the Board at LeverX, and Daniella Levine Cava, Mayor of Miami-Dade County.

  • Maximizing business efficiency: How leveraging SAP technology can unlock new levels of operational efficiency and enhance customer experiences.
  • AI in research & development: The role of AI in accelerating research and development processes, particularly in the pharmaceutical sector, driving faster innovation.
  • AI & digitalization for business sustainability: An exploration of how AI and digitalization are transforming business models, driving sustainability, and supporting growth in various industries.
  • Data modernization: The critical importance of modernizing data strategies to maintain a competitive edge in today’s rapidly evolving market.
  • Innovative service solutions: Multi-platform service applications that deliver enhanced operational capabilities and customer value were showcased.
  • Leadership & technology: How effective leadership can help organizations navigate technological advancements and leverage them for business growth.

🟢 Green Stage highlights

  • AI-powered Clean Core extensions: How SAP Business Technology Platform enhances key extensions with AI to drive innovation and operational efficiency.
  • Cybersecurity and AI: An exploration of the collaborative role of AI and Governance, Risk, and Compliance (GRC) solutions in improving SAP cybersecurity strategies.
  • Generative AI in enterprise operations: The benefits of integrating generative AI solutions from cloud platforms like AWS to transform enterprise operations.
  • ERP transformation success: A case study on the successful implementation of S/4HANA to streamline ERP transformation in the manufacturing sector.
  • Warehousing innovations: Emerging trends and innovations in SAP warehousing solutions that optimize supply chain processes.
  • Enhanced employee experiences: The discussion on how mobile technology, AI, and user-centered design to improve employee engagement and experiences.
  • HR transformation with SAP SuccessFactors: The journey of implementing SAP SuccessFactors to drive operational excellence in human resource management.
  • Digital transformation in the food industry: A real-world example of digital transformation within the food and beverage industry, focusing on improving operational efficiency and customer experience.

🟡Yellow Stage highlights

  • SAP PLM vision & strategy: An exploration of the latest developments and future strategies for SAP Product Lifecycle Management (PLM), focusing on innovation and business growth.
  • Global PLM implementation success: A real-world case study demonstrating the successful implementation of a global PLM system in discrete industries, highlighting its transformative effects on product lifecycle management.
  • Panel on PLM trends and challenges: A discussion of key trends in digital twins, product design, and the critical role of integrating PLM into enterprise resource planning (ERP) systems to drive business efficiency.

Breakout sessions:

  • Specification management & formulation: A deep dive into process PLM, focusing on the complexities of managing product specifications and formulation processes.
  • Integrating digital twins in PLM and ERP: An exploration of how digital twins are becoming an essential component of product design and how they integrate with SAP's PLM and ERP solutions.

 

As Day 2 of LEVEREDGE 2024 came to a close, a clear theme emerged: the critical role that AI, digital transformation, and innovative leadership play in shaping the future of business. As industries continue to innovate, the integration of AI, data-driven solutions, and leadership will remain key drivers of sustainable growth and transformation.

Key Highlights of Day 3

Day 3 of LEVEREDGE 2024 was all about embracing the transformative power of AI, digital transformation, and leadership innovation. As the final day of the event unfolded, two primary stages — the Blue Stage and the Yellow Stage (SAP PLM Forum) — hosted a range of insightful sessions. 

🔵 Blue Stage highlights

  • AI for business efficiency: Introduction to the practical integration of AI into business operations, focusing on enhancing efficiency and scalability.
  • AI in logistics: Insights on how AI is transforming logistics, improving delivery precision, and optimizing supply chain management.
  • Leadership and innovation: A session exploring the intersection of leadership and technology, offering lessons from leadership journeys and innovative business practices.
  • Digital transformation case studies: Real-world examples of how businesses are leveraging AI, mobile technology, and SAP solutions to drive digital transformation and operational excellence.
  • AWS and SAP BTP for innovation: Discussion on how AWS and SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) are enabling organizations to innovate and streamline processes for better business outcomes.
  • Mobile work order management: A demonstration of the technology behind mobile work order management, showcasing its ability to simplify workflows and enhance operational efficiency.

AI-powered solutions in action: Use cases illustrating how AI-powered solutions are driving industry transformation, with examples of businesses adopting AI to enhance their operations.

  • Digital transformation in transportation: Insights into how companies are using SAP S/4HANA and Transportation Management to achieve operational efficiencies in the transportation sector.
  • Maximizing SAP investments: An exploration of the strategic benefits of engaging with user groups to fully unlock the potential of SAP investments.
  • Managing vendor and supply chain risks: Best practices for navigating risks in vendor and supply chain management, with a focus on procurement and mitigation strategies.

🟡 Yellow Stage (SAP PLM Forum) highlights

  • Sustainable innovation: An exploration of how PLM drives innovation while ensuring sustainability and compliance.
  • AI in product development: How AI improves the efficiency of new product development processes.
  • PLM success stories: Real-world examples of PLM implementations, showcasing business transformation.

PLM security: Best practices for securing PLM applications and protecting data.

  • Product data governance: Managing product data and engineering changes effectively.
  • Industry-specific roadmaps: Future advancements in PLM for discrete and process industries.

As Day 3 concluded, a key takeaway was the undeniable impact of AI and digital transformation on industries across the board. From revolutionizing logistics and supply chains to driving sustainable innovation through PLM solutions, the sessions underscored how technology is reshaping modern business practices. The importance of leadership, collaboration, and continuous learning was evident, as attendees left with fresh perspectives and strategies to implement in their own organizations. LEVEREDGE 2024 concluded with a clear message: innovation, driven by AI and digital tools, is the future of business success.

The Bottom Line

LEVEREDGE 2024 showcased the future of business, emphasizing the pivotal role of AI, digital transformation, and leadership in driving innovation. Across three days of insightful sessions, industry leaders and experts shared their visions and practical strategies for navigating the evolving business landscape. From AI-powered logistics to sustainable PLM solutions, the event highlighted how technology is reshaping industries. As attendees leave equipped with fresh perspectives, the key takeaway is clear: embracing innovation and leveraging digital tools are essential for achieving lasting business success.

entry.title: Tailored IT strategy planning: The key to successfully harmonized business and IT goals
entry.content:
In recent conversations with various IT leaders on their projects and challenges, answers on the purpose of further IT implementations are, as expected, considerably multilayered. They range from “simple” IT cost-saving goals (TCO reduction) and security aspects (cybersecurity) to the adaptation, or even the creation of entirely new business models.  

The following diagram shows a snapshot of the challenges that companies routinely consider, divided across business-driven and underlying IT topics:

Additional concerns also emerged from these discussions, including complexity, silos, and a lack of coordinated plans across the different areas. They revealed that stakeholders underestimated the interdependence and need for coherent planning. Subprojects often start before realizing the importance in having a comprehensive plan, and as a result, the projects become inconsistent.

When working on a single project, one goal may be attainable throughout the project. However, when the project is part of a larger program or initiative, its shortcomings become transparent and overall target achievement is far removed. In addition to project delays, the initially saved effort – whether in time or money – leads to frustration among stakeholders who fulfilled their goals from their project perspective.

While a siloed focus on local interests is challenging, so is selecting one or more partners for the transformation. This is where the shortcomings of a missing coherent planning basis become clear. Decoupled subproject requests for anything (RFXs) result in individual quotations that fall short of building an overarching, comprehensive transformation plan.

Unraveling project challenges

Thinking beyond ready-made solutions and services and standardized procedures and delivery objects can be challenging when addressing complex issues. For that reason, a business transformation advisor can be beneficial. The advisor enables SAP customers to determine whether tailored services are necessary and which methods and tools are needed early in the full-scope planning process.

Like a tailor creating a custom suit, a business transformation advisor defines a tailored consulting service by using the following process: 

  1. Collect mandate and define scope: A clear definition of the mandate and scope should be approved and supported by management during the project planning phase. This involves all elements of the business strategy, including the mission, target markets, current and future product and service offerings, and sales and people strategies. Topics, such as AI and sustainability, must be appropriately considered – after all, plans are being made for the next 5 to 10 years. This helps ensure a holistic IT strategy is defined and aligned with all company goals.
  2. Recognize and understand the strategic direction: The intent and path forward must be carefully considered. Key questions include who represents the company strategy and its drivers in this endeavor, what decision criteria can be derived from the strategic direction of the business, and how the mandate’s objectives match the plan’s objectives.
  3. Define decision criteria: All subsequent steps and deliverables must align with the company's goals. Which deliverables will be required to make strategic decisions? How does the decision-making process for products and projects work? Who is involved, and what are their respective priorities and requirements?
  4. Define the procedure model: Picking suitable methods and tools for developing defined delivery objects is critical. Different criteria play a role in the selection, such as who can adequately represent the business and provide information about current needs and future projects, where knowledge can be found, and which tools are needed to analyze process performance and systems. In addition, the effectiveness of the chosen project methodology must be assessed in light of business circumstances.
  5. Determine success factors: Implementing measures to achieve goals and ensure compliance becomes increasingly difficult throughout the project when specific factors are unidentified, undefined, or poorly communicated. Critical factors include active management sponsorship, sufficient time and budget, potential resistance, and appropriate countermeasures. Above all, involving the right and most important partners – such as the primary software supplier – is essential, with their accountability being key.

The outcome of this process – when clearly aligned and tailored to business goals – forms a stable digital IT foundation for the company’s strategy. This foundation, in turn, is the basis for implementing the corporate strategy, by which a customer’s CIOs, chief digital officers (CDOs), and their teams are ultimately measured.

The next step is selecting appropriate partners for the implementation. The RFx can only be carried out successfully if a clear and solid basis has been developed. Specifically, this refers to the necessary deliverables and general conditions mentioned earlier to evaluate and compare offers meaningfully.

Companies often do not clearly define the essential elements of a transformation in their RFPs. Typical examples include an unclear process scope, undefined migration approach or exclusions, and lacking integration of third-party products and add-ons. As a result, quotations cannot be adequately compared, because they differ in content, effort estimates and price and are unaligned with the transformation plan.

Mapping the way forward

When defining a corporate strategy, questions often arise about SAP's role as a software provider. Our customers want to know we have the necessary industry, process, and methodology knowledge and neutrality to provide competent advice.

Business transformation advisors at SAP are exceptionally skilled in answering all those questions using flexible methods and tools developed, based on experiences with various customer projects. For example, advisors leverage SAP Signavio solutions, such as SAP Signavio Process Insights, for the initial process analysis. Then, to evaluate the target architecture, they rely on SAP LeanIX solutions and the SAP Reference Business Architecture tool. This toolbox is based on the TOGAF Standard enterprise architecture methodology with different layers, starting with the business architecture.

It is important to compare the business capabilities derived from the strategy with the SAP solutions and the involvement of required expertise. Such instances will occasionally welcome a special SAP product or industry expert, or even a software developer, who is brought in, in addition, to solve critical challenges. The products that reach the target architecture remain the customer’s decision, supported by SAP’s desire to remain neutral.

Company situations, strategic goals, decision-making processes, and adjustments to the scope, methodology, and service are always possible and naturally move customers away from a standard service. After a more intensive exchange, in my experience, all decision-makers prefer a tailored approach.

Establishing confidence in business changes

At the end of the project, business transformation advisors help ensure the results presented to the management and project team correspond to all necessary decision criteria. Demonstrating the usefulness and necessity of the cooperation between service design and project execution gives involved parties the confidence that the right decisions have been made.

This phase usually comprises the following main deliverables:

  • IT target architecture derived from the business strategy with associated requirements, primarily business capabilities, process scope, IT solutions, and the IT architecture
  • Compatible transformation road map to create a step-by-step and logical sequence while taking into account the dependencies and capacities for realization, such as a suitable deployment model
  • Effort estimate of all resources – including roles, people, and internal and external cost estimations – as well as a proposed cost for software procurement and its operation
  • Cost-benefit analysis, depending on the conditions of the decision-making process (value proposition or business case including ROI, NPV calculation)

Delivering business changes that matter

IT organizations have been trending, for years, toward initiating preliminary efforts as business projects supported by the entire leadership team. Through the involvement and collaboration of corporate management, as well as business and IT representatives in the early phase, this approach helps ensure all interests are considered as agreed and key players are involved and prepared for subsequent projects.

The positive change management aspect of this approach should not be underestimated. Affected employees at all levels should be involved early, especially those from the business. This is a vital success factor in the overall transformation.

The “cloud mindset” and “new way of working” are just two examples that demonstrate the importance of the people-change aspect of transformation. With the increasing footprint of cloud solutions, it is necessary to understand and accept the associated standardization and limitations to individualization. The benefits of combining new processes with modern interfaces are also not to be overlooked – these changes are a prerequisite for younger generations of employees in their daily work.

 

Ready to harmonize your business and IT goals by tailoring your IT strategy with a plan for a solid technology foundation? Read the overview “Your Success Is Our Priority.”
